key_insight: Compass improves prostate cancer detection by analyzing micro-ultrasound as a multi-view video stream rather than isolated frames, capturing rotational sweep context like expert readers do.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help clinicians better distinguish suspicious prostate regions during micro-ultrasound exams and complement human interpretation in prostate cancer diagnosis.
method: Models rotational sweep videos and biopsy-time micro-ultrasound frames together
Uses a transformer conditioned on probe rotational angle to aggregate evidence across the study
Outputs both frame-level and study-level risk scores
evidence_signal: The model was trained and evaluated on a multi-center clinical trial dataset of micro-ultrasound studies and compared against published AI baselines and expert risk scores.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality, well-registered video acquisition and multi-center validation before routine clinical deployment.
paper_title: Compass: Prostate Cancer Detection Needs Multi-View Context
